mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 200,000 jobs over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 200,000 jobs over 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS 3-month change and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth remains positive and above 2% year-over-year, indicating robust consumer demand that sustains corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as an always-on signal.
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth does not correlate with positive SPX returns (correlation < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining above 101.5, indicating a slowdown in manufacturing activity that signals weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of instances when INDPRO declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,400 thousand units while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 3.7%, indicating joint deterioration in housing demand and labor market tightness that signals recession risk and equity market stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction with housing and labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when both HOUST < 1,400 and UNRATE > 3.7%, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates to below 304.0 index points while the federal funds rate remains above 5.0%, creating a real rate squeeze that eventually forces policy easing and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in future rate cuts.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with disinflation momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL < 304.0 and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 3.8% in a single month, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers equity market repricing downward as investors reduce growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 3.8% in a single month
regime_hint: labor market inflection point or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE rises above 3.8% in a single month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 20,400 billion chained 2017 dollars with quarter-over-quarter growth positive, indicating sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as an always-on signal in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive real GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive SPX returns (correlation < 0.20).
